stories.title: Antarctic ice cores show ocean heat, not CO2, drove ancient climate shifts
stories.description: Two studies published Wednesday in Nature find that global ocean temperatures swung dramatically over 3 million years while greenhouse gas levels stayed broadly stable.

stories.title: Cuba vows 'impregnable resistance' after Trump threatens to 'take' island
stories.description: Diaz-Canel's defiance came as Cuba restored power after a 29-hour blackout tied to a U.S. oil blockade that has left the island in crisis.

stories.title: Micron reports earnings today after market cap tops $500B
stories.description: The memory chipmaker's stock has surged roughly 55% this year, driven by AI demand that has sold out its entire 2026 high-bandwidth memory output.

stories.title: Kremlin condemns 'murder' of Iran's Ali Larijani in U.S.-Israeli strikes
stories.description: Spokesman Dmitry Peskov called the killing of Iran's de facto wartime leader an attack on a "sovereign and independent" nation's leadership.

stories.title: Sen. Mullin faces hearing to lead Homeland Security amid DHS shutdown
stories.description: Trump's pick to replace fired DHS Secretary Kristi Noem testified Wednesday before the Senate committee, with a full confirmation vote possible next week.

stories.title: BP locks out 800 union workers at largest Midwest refinery
stories.description: The lockout at the Whiting, Indiana, facility takes effect March 19 after months of failed contract talks with United Steelworkers members.

stories.title: Israeli airstrikes kill at least six in central Beirut
stories.description: Israel's military campaign against Hezbollah intensified Wednesday with strikes flattening buildings in central Beirut and new evacuation orders in the south.

stories.title: Trump blasts NATO after allies reject Hormuz mission
stories.description: The president called the refusal "a very foolish mistake" while insisting the U.S. does not need help securing the strait Iran has shut down.

stories.title: Stratton wins Illinois Democratic Senate primary to replace Durbin
stories.description: Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton defeated Reps. Raja Krishnamoorthi and Robin Kelly despite being heavily outspent, NBC News projects.

stories.title: China pledges emergency aid to Iran, Jordan, Lebanon and Iraq
stories.description: Beijing announced humanitarian assistance for four Middle Eastern nations as the U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran enters its third week.

stories.title: Trump asks to delay Xi summit by a month over Iran war
stories.description: The president told reporters the ongoing conflict requires him to stay in Washington, pushing back a Beijing trip planned for late March.

stories.title: Pentagon says 157 killed in drug boat strikes since September
stories.description: A senior defense official told Congress that 47 suspected narco-trafficking vessels were destroyed across 45 strikes in the Caribbean and Eastern Pacific.

stories.title: Ukraine hits Moscow with drones for 4th straight day
stories.description: Russia says it intercepted 206 drones overnight, including 40 aimed at Moscow, as strikes also hit military aviation plants deep inside Russian territory.

stories.title: UK adviser believed Iran deal was in reach before US-led attack
stories.description: Britain's national security adviser judged Tehran's nuclear offer could have averted war, as most allies reject Trump's call to secure the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Democrats send DHS funding proposal amid month-long shutdown
stories.description: A top TSA official said the agency is "fully stretched" and may have to close airports as callout rates surge among 50,000 unpaid officers.

stories.title: Amazon CEO Jassy says AI could push AWS to $600B in annual sales by 2036
stories.description: The projection, made at an internal all-hands meeting, doubles Jassy's own prior estimate and implies a fivefold increase from AWS's 2025 revenue.

stories.title: Russia sharing satellite imagery, drone tech with Iran
stories.description: Moscow has expanded military cooperation to include satellite data and drone tactics refined during the Ukraine war, according to The Wall Street Journal.

stories.title: Roberts calls personal attacks on judges 'dangerous' after Trump tirade
stories.description: The chief justice urged critics across the political spectrum to stop targeting judges personally, days after Trump called the Supreme Court "inept."

stories.title: UK and Ukraine sign drone defense pact during Zelenskyy's London visit
stories.description: Zelenskyy met with Starmer, Rutte, and King Charles, urging allies to keep focus on Ukraine as the Iran war shifts global attention toward the Middle East.

stories.title: Moldova summons Russian envoy over oil spill from Ukraine strike
stories.description: Officials presented the ambassador with contaminated river water as cities remain without supply after a March 7 attack on a Ukrainian hydropower plant.

stories.title: Comer subpoenas AG Bondi over Epstein files handling
stories.description: The House Oversight chairman demands Bondi testify on April 14 about the DOJ's compliance with the Epstein Files Transparency Act.

stories.title: Pentagon raises Golden Dome cost to $185B to speed space systems
stories.description: The $10 billion increase will accelerate satellite tracking, sensor and communications programs central to the homeland missile defense shield.

stories.title: UC Irvine, Tel Aviv team build ion pump with no moving parts
stories.description: The ratchet-based device removes salt and heavy metals from water using only a low-voltage electrical signal, with no chemical reactions required.

stories.title: Ukraine accepts EU offer to fund Druzhba pipeline repairs
stories.description: The deal aims to break Hungary's veto on a €90 billion aid package for Kyiv ahead of this week's EU summit.

stories.title: Microsoft unifies Copilot teams, shifts Suleyman to superintelligence role
stories.description: CEO Satya Nadella merged commercial and consumer Copilot divisions under new EVP Jacob Andreou while redirecting AI chief Mustafa Suleyman to frontier model development.

stories.title: Pentagon begins engineering work to replace Anthropic AI
stories.description: The Defense Department's top AI official said integration of a new large language model is already underway as the military moves past Anthropic's Claude.

stories.title: Democrats introduce BETS OFF Act to ban prediction market trades on government actions
stories.description: The bill is the fifth proposal this year targeting the fast-growing industry, spurred by suspicious trading ahead of U.S. strikes on Iran.

stories.title: Mastercard to buy stablecoin firm BVNK for up to $1.8B
stories.description: The deal is the largest stablecoin acquisition ever, eclipsing Stripe's $1.1 billion purchase of Bridge in 2025.

stories.title: Russia vows solidarity with Cuba after Trump says he could 'take' the island
stories.description: Moscow condemned U.S. "interference" and pledged financial support to Havana after Trump told reporters he could do "anything I want" with Cuba.

stories.title: China's fuel export ban deepens Asia's energy crisis amid Iran war
stories.description: Beijing halted diesel, gasoline, and jet fuel exports as the Strait of Hormuz closure and Iran conflict drive prices sharply higher across the region.

stories.title: Top US Counterterrorism Official Joe Kent Resigns Over Iran War
stories.description: Kent accused Israeli officials of deploying "a misinformation campaign" that lured the U.S. into the conflict, now in its 17th day.

stories.title: AlphaFold Database adds millions of protein complex structures for the first time
stories.description: The expansion, a collaboration between EMBL-EBI, Google DeepMind, NVIDIA, and Seoul National University, targets proteins tied to human health and disease.

stories.title: Nebius plans $3.75B note offering after $27B Meta deal
stories.description: The AI cloud company aims to fund data center expansion and GPU procurement a day after announcing one of the industry's largest infrastructure contracts.

stories.title: Tropical peatland wildfires hit 2,000-year high, study finds
stories.description: Researchers analyzed charcoal records across four continents and linked the surge to drainage, deforestation and agricultural expansion in Southeast Asia.

stories.title: Japan, US to jointly develop rare earths and lithium at Thursday summit
stories.description: Mitsubishi Materials and Mitsui & Co. will participate in projects including a rare earth refinery in Indiana and a lithium mine in North Carolina.

stories.title: U.S. Navy awards Gecko Robotics $71M to deploy AI and robots across Pacific Fleet
stories.description: The five-year contract, the Navy's largest robotics deal, will use wall-climbing robots to inspect 18 ships and help reach 80% fleet readiness by 2027.

stories.title: NASA taps Mars rover to watch the Sun ahead of Artemis II launch
stories.description: The Perseverance rover can spot sunspots two weeks early from Mars, giving mission teams advance warning as the April 1 launch window nears.

stories.title: Fire on USS Gerald R. Ford burned 30 hours, displaced 600 sailors
stories.description: A New York Times report reveals the full scope of the blaze aboard the carrier, now approaching 10 months at sea in the Red Sea.

stories.title: Taliban says Pakistani airstrike on Kabul hospital killed 400
stories.description: The strike hit a 2,000-bed drug rehabilitation facility Monday evening, marking one of the deadliest attacks in the weeks-long conflict between the neighbors.

stories.title: UCAR sues to block dismantling of NCAR weather research center
stories.description: The 129-university consortium filed a federal lawsuit alleging political retaliation and constitutional violations over the Trump administration's bid to break up the facility.

stories.title: U.S. transfers sacred Oak Flat land to Rio Tinto-BHP copper venture
stories.description: The Forest Service handed over 2,400 acres of Arizona's Tonto National Forest, clearing the way for one of North America's largest copper mines despite tribal opposition.

stories.title: Zambia balks at US deal tying HIV aid to mineral access
stories.description: The draft agreement would cut health funding while requiring mining concessions, data sharing, and domestic co-investment far exceeding terms offered to other African nations.

stories.title: Kremlin denies peace process is fading but no new talks are set
stories.description: Spokesman Peskov says Trump hasn't lost interest in Ukraine negotiations, even as the U.S. focus on Iran leaves talks without a date or venue.

stories.title: Iran's Mojtaba Khamenei names ex-IRGC chief as military adviser
stories.description: Mohsen Rezaei, a hardline former Revolutionary Guards commander, takes the role amid Iran's escalating conflict with the U.S. and Israel.

stories.title: Judge blocks RFK Jr.'s childhood vaccine schedule overhaul
stories.description: A federal judge said medical groups are likely to prove that Kennedy’s overhaul of the childhood vaccine schedule and his replacement of the CDC’s vaccine advisory panel violated federal law.

stories.title: Rubio orders diplomats to press allies for action against Iran
stories.description: The directive, in a private cable obtained by ABC News, sets a March 20 deadline as the U.S.-Iran war enters its third week.

stories.title: Ukraine hits oil depot 500 km from front in latest Krasnodar Krai strike
stories.description: The Labinsk attack caps a week of escalating drone strikes on southern Russia's energy network, including hits on the Tikhoretsk hub and Afipsky refinery.

stories.title: House passes bill to expand sanctions on Iran's oil trade
stories.description: The measure targets foreign entities facilitating Iranian oil exports but was softened at the Trump administration's request, according to Jewish Insider.

stories.title: EU sanctions 16 Iranian officials over January protest crackdown
stories.description: The measures target IRGC commanders, judiciary members, and a surveillance tech firm, bringing EU human rights sanctions on Iran to 263 individuals and 53 entities.

stories.title: Last detained Columbia protester freed after a year in ICE custody
stories.description: Leqaa Kordia was released Monday after the government declined to challenge a judge's third order for her release on $100,000 bond.

stories.title: Vance publicly defends Trump's Iran war, dismisses rift reports
stories.description: The vice president called Trump a "smart president" and said he trusts him to avoid past mistakes, despite his own history of opposing foreign wars.

stories.title: EU sanctions 13 over Bucha massacre and Kremlin disinformation
stories.description: The measures target nine Russian military officers linked to the 2022 killings and four individuals accused of spreading pro-Kremlin propaganda.

stories.title: Rubio designates Iranian American prisoner as wrongfully detained
stories.description: Kamran Hekmati, jailed for visiting Israel for his son's bar mitzvah over a decade ago, is now the second American given the formal designation.

stories.title: Trump discloses Rep. Dunn's terminal heart diagnosis at White House event
stories.description: The president revealed the Florida Republican would have been "dead by June" without emergency surgery, catching Speaker Johnson off guard.

stories.title: Drone strike sparks fire at Abu Dhabi's Shah oil field
stories.description: The attack is the latest in a series of Iranian strikes on Gulf energy infrastructure since the U.S.-Israel war with Iran began in early March.

stories.title: US and Iran reestablish direct contact amid ongoing conflict
stories.description: Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi exchanged text messages, the first known direct communication since the war began Feb. 28.

stories.title: DOJ proposes rule to help states fast-track executions
stories.description: The proposed change would ease certification requirements under a 1996 law that no state has ever successfully used to expedite federal death penalty reviews.

stories.title: All five DNA and RNA building blocks found in asteroid Ryugu samples
stories.description: A Nature Astronomy study reports the complete set of nucleobases in Ryugu material, making it the second asteroid confirmed to carry life's genetic ingredients.

stories.title: Trump says he'll have the 'honor of taking Cuba'
stories.description: The president's most aggressive remarks yet on Cuba came during a White House event, as both governments have confirmed ongoing diplomatic talks.

stories.title: Cuba to let expatriates invest in and own businesses on the island
stories.description: Deputy Prime Minister Fraga announced the reforms in an NBC News interview, as the island faces its worst energy and economic crisis in decades.

stories.title: Starmer says Hormuz reopening effort won't be NATO-led
stories.description: The UK is building a broad coalition with Gulf, European, and U.S. partners to restore shipping through the strait, which Iran has effectively blockaded.

stories.title: Susie Wiles diagnosed with early-stage breast cancer
stories.description: Trump announced the diagnosis on Truth Social, saying his chief of staff has an "excellent" prognosis and will keep working during treatment.

stories.title: Greece joins Germany in refusing military role at Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Athens said it will only participate in the EU's Red Sea mission as EU foreign ministers meet Monday to discuss expanding the Aspides operation.

stories.title: Oxford study identifies new class of molten exoplanet
stories.description: L 98-59 d, just 35 light-years away, harbors a permanent magma ocean that stores sulfur and defies existing planetary categories, researchers say.

stories.title: Fifth Iranian player abandons asylum, leaving two in Australia
stories.description: Only two of seven Iranian women's soccer players who accepted humanitarian visas remain after a fifth member departed overnight Sunday.

stories.title: Rare Level 4 severe weather risk targets East Coast as storm outbreak continues Monday
stories.description: Tornadoes, damaging winds and blizzards have already destroyed homes and grounded hundreds of flights, with the worst expected Monday in the mid-Atlantic.

stories.title: Iran war hits $12B as Tehran rejects ceasefire talks
stories.description: White House adviser Kevin Hassett disclosed the cost on Sunday as Iran's foreign minister denied requesting a ceasefire, contradicting Trump's claims.

stories.title: 'One Battle After Another' wins best picture at 98th Academy Awards
stories.description: Paul Thomas Anderson's political thriller took home six Oscars, including best director and adapted screenplay, in his first Academy Award wins.

stories.title: Iran's new supreme leader reportedly flown to Moscow for surgery
stories.description: Al-Jarida reports Mojtaba Khamenei was evacuated to Russia on a military aircraft after being wounded in the Feb. 28 strikes that killed his father.

stories.title: Trump accuses Iran of using AI to fake war victories, threatens media with treason
stories.description: The president claimed Tehran is flooding social media with fabricated images and videos to mask battlefield losses, and said outlets amplifying the content should face charges.

stories.title: IDF says Michigan synagogue attacker's brother was Hezbollah commander
stories.description: Israel's military disclosed that Ibrahim Ghazali led weapons operations for Hezbollah's Badr Unit before being killed in the same airstrike that preceded the attack.

stories.title: House Republicans vow to block Senate bills until SAVE Act advances
stories.description: About 40 to 50 House GOP members threaten to halt all Senate legislation unless Thune brings the voter ID bill to a vote.

stories.title: China unveils robotic workforce for lunar base construction by 2035
stories.description: Researchers proposed a wheeled semi-humanoid robot and a six-legged cargo platform to support the planned International Lunar Research Station.

stories.title: Missile hits U.S. Embassy compound in Baghdad as Americans told to leave Iraq
stories.description: The State Department urged all U.S. citizens to depart immediately, warning that Iran-aligned militias pose an ongoing threat across Iraq.

stories.title: GOP Sen. Johnson breaks with Trump over FCC license threats tied to Iran war coverage
stories.description: FCC Chair Brendan Carr's warning that broadcasters could lose licenses over Iran war reporting drew rare bipartisan rebuke, including from a Republican senator.

stories.title: North Korea fires 12 nuclear-capable rockets as US-South Korea drills continue
stories.description: Kim Jong Un and his daughter oversaw the live-fire drill Saturday, which Pyongyang called 100% accurate amid rising tensions over joint military exercises.

stories.title: Families identify airmen killed in KC-135 crash in Iraq
stories.description: Maj. Alex Klinner, 33, and Tech. Sgt. Tyler Simmons, 28, are among six crew members killed in the March 12 midair collision during Operation Epic Fury.

stories.title: White House tightens control over RFK Jr.'s HHS amid midterm fears
stories.description: Aides moved to curb Kennedy's anti-vaccine focus after internal polling found it deeply unpopular, even among MAHA supporters.

stories.title: Moscow businesses reel as mobile internet blackout enters second week
stories.description: Russia's capital has lost widespread mobile connectivity since March 5, with analysts calling it the largest test yet of a government "whitelist" censorship system.

stories.title: EU extends Russia sanctions until September despite Hungary, Slovakia resistance
stories.description: Around 2,600 individuals and entities, including Putin, remain subject to travel bans and asset freezes after a last-minute deal.

stories.title: MAGA Inc. sits on $300M war chest as GOP awaits Trump's go-ahead
stories.description: Republicans worry Trump may not deploy his super PAC's record funds to protect vulnerable House members ahead of November's midterms.

stories.title: IDF says it killed two Iranian intelligence chiefs in Tehran airstrike
stories.description: Both officers had been appointed after their predecessor was eliminated in earlier strikes during Operation Roaring Lion.

stories.title: UAE consulate in Erbil hit by drone for second time in a week
stories.description: Two security personnel were wounded in Saturday's strike, which the UAE called an "unprovoked terrorist drone attack" and a violation of international law.

stories.title: Lobbyist who helped client get Trump pardon charged with extortion
stories.description: Joshua Nass allegedly hired an "enforcer" to threaten the client's son into paying $500,000 he claimed was owed for lobbying services.

stories.title: Iranians' regime change hopes fade as bombing enters third week
stories.description: Tehran residents who cheered U.S.-Israeli strikes now face a battered but defiant security state and a new supreme leader consolidating power.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill six in Gaza as ceasefire nears fifth month
stories.description: Gaza's civil defense agency says a pre-dawn strike in Khan Younis and a separate attack in Gaza City killed six people in the past 24 hours.

stories.title: Explosion hits Amsterdam Jewish school in 'deliberate attack'
stories.description: Mayor Femke Halsema condemned the overnight blast as a "cowardly act of aggression" amid a wave of attacks on European Jewish sites.

stories.title: Over 52,000 flights canceled as Middle East crisis enters third week
stories.description: An estimated six million passengers have been affected since the conflict began Feb. 28, with cancellation rates only now starting to ease.

stories.title: Hamas urges Iran to stop striking Gulf neighbors
stories.description: The Iran-backed group publicly distanced itself from Tehran's regional attacks for the first time since the war began, amid threats of expulsion from Qatar.

stories.title: DC-area airports reopen after chemical odor halts flights for hours
stories.description: An overheated circuit board at a Virginia air traffic control facility forced ground stops at four airports, causing delays stretching past midnight.

stories.title: Greek tanker hit near Russia's Black Sea port
stories.description: Greece's shipping minister called the strike on the Maran Homer "unacceptable," vowing protests at the European Council level.

stories.title: Revolution Wind delivers first power to New England grid after surviving two Trump shutdowns
stories.description: The $6.2 billion offshore wind farm off Rhode Island will supply 350,000 homes at roughly a third of current regional electricity prices.

stories.title: Trump blames 'genetics' of attackers after wave of violence
stories.description: The president linked recent attacks in New York, Virginia, and Michigan to assailants' genetics, drawing criticism from experts who call the language eugenics-adjacent.

stories.title: Iran's UN envoy warns European bases could be targets
stories.description: Ambassador Ali Bahreini told Euronews that any facility used to attack Iran, including US bases in Europe, is a "legitimate target."

stories.title: Qatar says food reserves can last 18 months amid Iran war
stories.description: The interior minister said water supplies cover four months and medicine stocks nine months as Iranian strikes continue across the Gulf.

stories.title: USDA says it's close to announcing fertilizer relief for farmers
stories.description: Agriculture Secretary Brook Rollins said Friday her department is exploring "every potential avenue" to curb costs as the Iran war leaves a 25% supply gap.

stories.title: Cuba says FBI team to help probe deadly speedboat incursion
stories.description: Cuban President Díaz-Canel confirmed the FBI will send investigators to Havana, marking rare cooperation amid strained U.S.-Cuba relations.

stories.title: UN says nearly all Gaza aid movements denied in past two days
stories.description: Only one humanitarian mission was allowed through to the Kerem Shalom crossing on Thursday and Friday, enabling fuel collection "but nothing else."

stories.title: Qatar orders evacuations as Gulf conflict enters third week
stories.description: Debris from an intercepted drone struck Dubai's financial district Friday as Qatar began evacuating residents from specified areas overnight.

stories.title: Colombia sends ministers to Caracas after presidential summit abruptly canceled
stories.description: A planned border meeting between Presidents Petro and Rodriguez was called off for undisclosed reasons, shifting talks to the ministerial level.

stories.title: Iran-backed Iraqi militia offers bounty on US officials
stories.description: The Islamic Resistance in Iraq announced a roughly $114,000 reward for intelligence leading to the capture or killing of senior American personnel.

stories.title: Kansas residents split over Deep Fission's underground nuclear reactor project
stories.description: Dozens gathered at a March 12 public meeting in Parsons, Kansas, days after the startup began drilling for its first-of-a-kind underground reactor.

stories.title: UAE charges 21 people for filming Iranian missile strikes
stories.description: Detainees include a British tourist and an Indian student, as the Gulf state cracks down on sharing footage of drone and missile attacks.

stories.title: Iranian strike damages 5 US refueling planes at Saudi base
stories.description: The planes at Prince Sultan Air Base are being repaired with no casualties reported, according to The Wall Street Journal.

stories.title: Schumer demands Bessent testify over Russia sanctions relief
stories.description: Senate Democrats condemned the Treasury Department's decision to temporarily lift sanctions on Russian oil stranded at sea amid the Iran conflict.

stories.title: US seeks to sell seized tanker after $47M in costs
stories.description: Federal prosecutors asked a judge Thursday for permission to sell a confiscated Venezuelan oil tanker and its cargo after maintenance costs far exceeded the ship's value.

stories.title: Zelensky calls Ukraine-Russia peace talks a 'soap opera' as venue dispute stalls process
stories.description: The U.S., Ukraine and Russia cannot agree on where or when to meet, with the Iran war keeping American officials from traveling abroad.

stories.title: South Korea's PM meets Trump after $350B investment bill clears parliament
stories.description: The unannounced White House meeting followed the National Assembly's passage of legislation enabling Seoul to fulfill its U.S. investment pledge.

stories.title: Gulf states lose $15B in energy revenue as Hormuz blockade nears third week
stories.description: The Financial Times reports Saudi Arabia alone has lost $4.5 billion, with $10.7 billion in oil and LNG cargo stranded on ships unable to depart.

stories.title: ROTC students killed gunman who attacked Old Dominion University classroom, FBI says
stories.description: The FBI is investigating the shooting as terrorism after a convicted ISIS supporter killed an ROTC instructor and wounded two others Thursday.

stories.title: Judge blocks Trump from forcing colleges to submit race data
stories.description: A federal judge in Boston extended the compliance deadline to March 25 after 17 Democratic state attorneys general sued to halt the requirement.

stories.title: Finland won't host nuclear arms in peacetime, Stubb says
stories.description: President Stubb sought to ease tensions after Finland's government last week moved to repeal a decades-old ban on nuclear weapons within its borders.

stories.title: Over 50,000 Americans return from Middle East since Iran war began
stories.description: The State Department said Friday that demand for charter flights is declining as the evacuation effort enters its third week.

stories.title: Judge orders indefinite funding for CFPB, rebuking Trump administration
stories.description: A California federal judge found officials used flawed legal reasoning in a "transparent attempt" to shut down the consumer watchdog agency.

stories.title: Sens. Warren and Van Hollen demand Hegseth's firing over Iran school strike
stories.description: The Democratic senators say the defense secretary's rollback of civilian harm protections contributed to the deadly Feb. 28 bombing of an elementary school.

stories.title: Judge blocks DOJ subpoenas targeting Fed Chair Powell
stories.description: The ruling found "zero evidence" of a crime and called the probe a pretext to pressure Powell on interest rates or force his resignation.

stories.title: UAE says Iran must halt attacks before any mediation can begin
stories.description: Diplomat Lana Nusseibeh said the guns must go silent first but expressed confidence Trump will lead to a negotiated end to the war.

stories.title: Accused drug kingpin Marset arrested in Bolivia, flown to US
stories.description: Sebastián Marset, known as "the King of the South" and a top DEA fugitive, was captured in a predawn raid in Santa Cruz de la Sierra.

stories.title: Pakistan bombs Kandahar fuel depot, Kabul homes, ending week-long lull
stories.description: Overnight strikes killed six people, including children, according to the Taliban, shattering a fragile pause achieved through Chinese mediation.

stories.title: Federal judge blocks Trump plan to end TPS for Somali immigrants
stories.description: The temporary restraining order preserves legal protections for about 1,100 Somali nationals days before a March 17 termination deadline.

stories.title: Midwest farmers rush to sell stored grain as Iran war fuels price surge
stories.description: Corn and soybean futures have each climbed roughly 6% since the conflict began, with soybeans topping $12 per bushel for the first time since mid-2024.

stories.title: Cuba confirms opening talks with U.S. after months of denial
stories.description: President Diaz-Canel said he directed negotiations alongside Raul Castro, marking Havana's first official acknowledgment of discussions with Washington.

stories.title: Trump rejected Putin's offer to move Iran's uranium to Russia
stories.description: The proposal, made during a March 9 phone call, aimed to resolve the dispute over Iran's enriched uranium stockpile as part of a broader war settlement.

stories.title: US military's AI-powered system struck 6,000 targets in Iran as war enters third week
stories.description: The Maven Smart System, built by Palantir and powered by Anthropic's Claude, compressed weeks of targeting work into hours amid growing civilian harm concerns.

stories.title: Sri Lanka repatriates remains of 84 Iranian sailors killed in US submarine attack
stories.description: The bodies were flown from Mattala airport nine days after a US torpedo sank the frigate IRIS Dena off Sri Lanka's southern coast.

stories.title: Japan set to join Trump's Golden Dome missile defense program
stories.description: PM Takaichi plans to announce Japan's participation when she meets Trump in Washington on March 19, according to Japanese government sources.

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israeli strikes killed top Iranian nuclear scientist
stories.description: The claim came during his first press conference since the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran began Feb. 28, now in its 14th day.

stories.title: Scientists decode 300-million-year-old DNA code hidden in plant genomes
stories.description: A massive study of 284 plant species identified 2.3 million conserved regulatory sequences, some predating flowering plants entirely.

stories.title: Trump signs orders to cut housing regulations, ease mortgage access
stories.description: The executive orders aim to speed up construction permitting and bring community banks back into mortgage lending ahead of the midterms.

stories.title: Japan's Katayama signals readiness to act on yen as currency nears 160
stories.description: Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said authorities will take "all necessary measures" as the yen hit its weakest level since July 2024 amid surging oil prices.

stories.title: ByteDance taps Malaysia partner to deploy 36,000 Nvidia Blackwell chips
stories.description: The TikTok parent is working with Aolani Cloud to build AI computing capacity outside China, skirting U.S. export controls on direct chip sales.

stories.title: Democrats warn new Trump tariffs will cost households $2,512 this year
stories.description: A Joint Economic Committee study released Friday says the post-Supreme Court tariff regime represents a 44% increase over 2025 household costs.

stories.title: Florida passes bill requiring proof of citizenship to vote
stories.description: The legislation, which won't take effect until 2027, also eliminates student and retirement community IDs as acceptable forms of voter identification.

stories.title: Democrats unveil bills to cut taxes for workers, raise them on millionaires
stories.description: Federal legislation introduced by Sen. Van Hollen would eliminate income taxes for those earning under $46,000, funded by surtaxes on millionaires.

stories.title: GOP senators tell Americans to accept higher gas prices as cost of Iran war
stories.description: Gas has surged roughly 20% since the conflict began, with the national average hitting $3.58 a gallon as Strait of Hormuz disruptions roil oil markets.

stories.title: NASA clears Artemis II for April 1 moon launch
stories.description: Four astronauts will orbit the moon for the first time since Apollo 17, following a flight readiness review that resolved months of technical delays.

stories.title: Iran warns regional blackout would follow any US grid strike
stories.description: Tehran's top security official said darkness would let Iran "hunt down US servicemen," as threats expanded to oil and energy targets.

stories.title: Drones hit Dubai again as Iran's Gulf attacks enter 13th day
stories.description: Multiple drone incidents struck Dubai overnight and Thursday morning, including near the financial district, as corporate evacuations spread across the region.

stories.title: Aramco denies reports of talks to buy Ukrainian drones
stories.description: The Saudi oil giant called a Wall Street Journal report about negotiations with Ukrainian interceptor drone makers "inaccurate," saying no such procurement talks are underway.

stories.title: Polish president vetoes €44B EU defense loan bill
stories.description: President Nawrocki rejected legislation unlocking EU funds under the SAFE program, calling it a threat to sovereignty and sparking a clash with PM Tusk.

stories.title: Iran's UN envoy says strait won't close, contradicting new supreme leader
stories.description: Mojtaba Khamenei's first statement vowed to keep the Strait of Hormuz shut, but Iran's UN ambassador said hours later Tehran would not block the waterway.

stories.title: Israel using tips from Iranian civilians to guide Tehran drone strikes
stories.description: A senior Israeli official says ordinary Iranians are submitting target locations via social media, helping guide strikes on Basij checkpoints in Tehran.

stories.title: Greene says GOP 'hijacked' by hawks as MAGA splits over Iran war
stories.description: The former congresswoman accused party leaders of enabling a neoconservative takeover, deepening fractures within Trump's coalition over the two-week-old conflict.

stories.title: US envoy rebukes Czechs for falling short of NATO spending target
stories.description: Czech lawmakers approved a 2026 budget allocating just 1.7% of GDP to defense, well below NATO's 5% goal agreed at The Hague Summit.

stories.title: Israel says it struck nuclear weapons site in Tehran
stories.description: The IDF said the Taleghan compound was tied to Iran's covert AMAD nuclear weapons program, as the war enters its third week.

stories.title: US reopens DEA office in Dominican Republic after corruption probe
stories.description: The Santo Domingo office was shuttered in February after a senior agent was arrested on bribery and visa fraud charges.

stories.title: Canada says Iran war will dominate upcoming G7 foreign ministers' meeting
stories.description: Foreign Affairs Minister Anita Anand said G7 allies will hold "a full-fledged conversation" on diplomatic efforts when they gather near Paris on March 26-27.

stories.title: Israel attacked Iran with no regime change plan, security sources say
stories.description: Nearly two weeks into the U.S.-Israeli bombing campaign, senior Israeli officials admit hopes of a popular uprising were based on "wishful thinking."

stories.title: Israel drops charges against soldiers in Sde Teiman sexual abuse case
stories.description: Netanyahu praised the decision, saying Israel "should pursue its adversaries, not its heroic fighters." Rights groups condemned it as impunity.

stories.title: Israeli drone strike on Lebanese University kills two senior academics
stories.description: The faculty of sciences director and a professor were killed on the Hadath campus near Beirut's southern suburbs amid intensified Israeli bombardment across Lebanon.

top.title: Antarctic ice cores show ocean heat, not CO2, drove ancient climate shifts
top.description: Two studies published Wednesday in Nature find that global ocean temperatures swung dramatically over 3 million years while greenhouse gas levels stayed broadly stable.

top.title: Cuba vows 'impregnable resistance' after Trump threatens to 'take' island
top.description: Diaz-Canel's defiance came as Cuba restored power after a 29-hour blackout tied to a U.S. oil blockade that has left the island in crisis.

top.title: Micron reports earnings today after market cap tops $500B
top.description: The memory chipmaker's stock has surged roughly 55% this year, driven by AI demand that has sold out its entire 2026 high-bandwidth memory output.

top.title: Kremlin condemns 'murder' of Iran's Ali Larijani in U.S.-Israeli strikes
top.description: Spokesman Dmitry Peskov called the killing of Iran's de facto wartime leader an attack on a "sovereign and independent" nation's leadership.

top.title: Sen. Mullin faces hearing to lead Homeland Security amid DHS shutdown
top.description: Trump's pick to replace fired DHS Secretary Kristi Noem testified Wednesday before the Senate committee, with a full confirmation vote possible next week.

top.title: BP locks out 800 union workers at largest Midwest refinery
top.description: The lockout at the Whiting, Indiana, facility takes effect March 19 after months of failed contract talks with United Steelworkers members.

top.title: Israeli airstrikes kill at least six in central Beirut
top.description: Israel's military campaign against Hezbollah intensified Wednesday with strikes flattening buildings in central Beirut and new evacuation orders in the south.

top.title: Trump blasts NATO after allies reject Hormuz mission
top.description: The president called the refusal "a very foolish mistake" while insisting the U.S. does not need help securing the strait Iran has shut down.

top.title: Stratton wins Illinois Democratic Senate primary to replace Durbin
top.description: Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton defeated Reps. Raja Krishnamoorthi and Robin Kelly despite being heavily outspent, NBC News projects.

top.title: China pledges emergency aid to Iran, Jordan, Lebanon and Iraq
top.description: Beijing announced humanitarian assistance for four Middle Eastern nations as the U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran enters its third week.

top.title: Trump asks to delay Xi summit by a month over Iran war
top.description: The president told reporters the ongoing conflict requires him to stay in Washington, pushing back a Beijing trip planned for late March.

top.title: Pentagon says 157 killed in drug boat strikes since September
top.description: A senior defense official told Congress that 47 suspected narco-trafficking vessels were destroyed across 45 strikes in the Caribbean and Eastern Pacific.

top.title: Ukraine hits Moscow with drones for 4th straight day
top.description: Russia says it intercepted 206 drones overnight, including 40 aimed at Moscow, as strikes also hit military aviation plants deep inside Russian territory.

top.title: UK adviser believed Iran deal was in reach before US-led attack
top.description: Britain's national security adviser judged Tehran's nuclear offer could have averted war, as most allies reject Trump's call to secure the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: Democrats send DHS funding proposal amid month-long shutdown
top.description: A top TSA official said the agency is "fully stretched" and may have to close airports as callout rates surge among 50,000 unpaid officers.

top.title: Amazon CEO Jassy says AI could push AWS to $600B in annual sales by 2036
top.description: The projection, made at an internal all-hands meeting, doubles Jassy's own prior estimate and implies a fivefold increase from AWS's 2025 revenue.

top.title: Russia sharing satellite imagery, drone tech with Iran
top.description: Moscow has expanded military cooperation to include satellite data and drone tactics refined during the Ukraine war, according to The Wall Street Journal.

top.title: Roberts calls personal attacks on judges 'dangerous' after Trump tirade
top.description: The chief justice urged critics across the political spectrum to stop targeting judges personally, days after Trump called the Supreme Court "inept."

top.title: UK and Ukraine sign drone defense pact during Zelenskyy's London visit
top.description: Zelenskyy met with Starmer, Rutte, and King Charles, urging allies to keep focus on Ukraine as the Iran war shifts global attention toward the Middle East.

top.title: Moldova summons Russian envoy over oil spill from Ukraine strike
top.description: Officials presented the ambassador with contaminated river water as cities remain without supply after a March 7 attack on a Ukrainian hydropower plant.

top.title: Comer subpoenas AG Bondi over Epstein files handling
top.description: The House Oversight chairman demands Bondi testify on April 14 about the DOJ's compliance with the Epstein Files Transparency Act.

top.title: Pentagon raises Golden Dome cost to $185B to speed space systems
top.description: The $10 billion increase will accelerate satellite tracking, sensor and communications programs central to the homeland missile defense shield.

top.title: UC Irvine, Tel Aviv team build ion pump with no moving parts
top.description: The ratchet-based device removes salt and heavy metals from water using only a low-voltage electrical signal, with no chemical reactions required.

top.title: Ukraine accepts EU offer to fund Druzhba pipeline repairs
top.description: The deal aims to break Hungary's veto on a €90 billion aid package for Kyiv ahead of this week's EU summit.

top.title: Microsoft unifies Copilot teams, shifts Suleyman to superintelligence role
top.description: CEO Satya Nadella merged commercial and consumer Copilot divisions under new EVP Jacob Andreou while redirecting AI chief Mustafa Suleyman to frontier model development.

top.title: Pentagon begins engineering work to replace Anthropic AI
top.description: The Defense Department's top AI official said integration of a new large language model is already underway as the military moves past Anthropic's Claude.

top.title: Democrats introduce BETS OFF Act to ban prediction market trades on government actions
top.description: The bill is the fifth proposal this year targeting the fast-growing industry, spurred by suspicious trading ahead of U.S. strikes on Iran.

top.title: Mastercard to buy stablecoin firm BVNK for up to $1.8B
top.description: The deal is the largest stablecoin acquisition ever, eclipsing Stripe's $1.1 billion purchase of Bridge in 2025.

top.title: Russia vows solidarity with Cuba after Trump says he could 'take' the island
top.description: Moscow condemned U.S. "interference" and pledged financial support to Havana after Trump told reporters he could do "anything I want" with Cuba.

top.title: China's fuel export ban deepens Asia's energy crisis amid Iran war
top.description: Beijing halted diesel, gasoline, and jet fuel exports as the Strait of Hormuz closure and Iran conflict drive prices sharply higher across the region.

top.title: Top US Counterterrorism Official Joe Kent Resigns Over Iran War
top.description: Kent accused Israeli officials of deploying "a misinformation campaign" that lured the U.S. into the conflict, now in its 17th day.

top.title: AlphaFold Database adds millions of protein complex structures for the first time
top.description: The expansion, a collaboration between EMBL-EBI, Google DeepMind, NVIDIA, and Seoul National University, targets proteins tied to human health and disease.

top.title: Nebius plans $3.75B note offering after $27B Meta deal
top.description: The AI cloud company aims to fund data center expansion and GPU procurement a day after announcing one of the industry's largest infrastructure contracts.

top.title: Tropical peatland wildfires hit 2,000-year high, study finds
top.description: Researchers analyzed charcoal records across four continents and linked the surge to drainage, deforestation and agricultural expansion in Southeast Asia.

top.title: Japan, US to jointly develop rare earths and lithium at Thursday summit
top.description: Mitsubishi Materials and Mitsui & Co. will participate in projects including a rare earth refinery in Indiana and a lithium mine in North Carolina.

top.title: U.S. Navy awards Gecko Robotics $71M to deploy AI and robots across Pacific Fleet
top.description: The five-year contract, the Navy's largest robotics deal, will use wall-climbing robots to inspect 18 ships and help reach 80% fleet readiness by 2027.

top.title: NASA taps Mars rover to watch the Sun ahead of Artemis II launch
top.description: The Perseverance rover can spot sunspots two weeks early from Mars, giving mission teams advance warning as the April 1 launch window nears.

top.title: Fire on USS Gerald R. Ford burned 30 hours, displaced 600 sailors
top.description: A New York Times report reveals the full scope of the blaze aboard the carrier, now approaching 10 months at sea in the Red Sea.

top.title: Taliban says Pakistani airstrike on Kabul hospital killed 400
top.description: The strike hit a 2,000-bed drug rehabilitation facility Monday evening, marking one of the deadliest attacks in the weeks-long conflict between the neighbors.

top.title: UCAR sues to block dismantling of NCAR weather research center
top.description: The 129-university consortium filed a federal lawsuit alleging political retaliation and constitutional violations over the Trump administration's bid to break up the facility.

top.title: U.S. transfers sacred Oak Flat land to Rio Tinto-BHP copper venture
top.description: The Forest Service handed over 2,400 acres of Arizona's Tonto National Forest, clearing the way for one of North America's largest copper mines despite tribal opposition.

top.title: Zambia balks at US deal tying HIV aid to mineral access
top.description: The draft agreement would cut health funding while requiring mining concessions, data sharing, and domestic co-investment far exceeding terms offered to other African nations.

top.title: Kremlin denies peace process is fading but no new talks are set
top.description: Spokesman Peskov says Trump hasn't lost interest in Ukraine negotiations, even as the U.S. focus on Iran leaves talks without a date or venue.

top.title: Iran's Mojtaba Khamenei names ex-IRGC chief as military adviser
top.description: Mohsen Rezaei, a hardline former Revolutionary Guards commander, takes the role amid Iran's escalating conflict with the U.S. and Israel.

top.title: Judge blocks RFK Jr.'s childhood vaccine schedule overhaul
top.description: A federal judge said medical groups are likely to prove that Kennedy’s overhaul of the childhood vaccine schedule and his replacement of the CDC’s vaccine advisory panel violated federal law.

top.title: Rubio orders diplomats to press allies for action against Iran
top.description: The directive, in a private cable obtained by ABC News, sets a March 20 deadline as the U.S.-Iran war enters its third week.

top.title: Ukraine hits oil depot 500 km from front in latest Krasnodar Krai strike
top.description: The Labinsk attack caps a week of escalating drone strikes on southern Russia's energy network, including hits on the Tikhoretsk hub and Afipsky refinery.

top.title: House passes bill to expand sanctions on Iran's oil trade
top.description: The measure targets foreign entities facilitating Iranian oil exports but was softened at the Trump administration's request, according to Jewish Insider.

top.title: EU sanctions 16 Iranian officials over January protest crackdown
top.description: The measures target IRGC commanders, judiciary members, and a surveillance tech firm, bringing EU human rights sanctions on Iran to 263 individuals and 53 entities.

top.title: Last detained Columbia protester freed after a year in ICE custody
top.description: Leqaa Kordia was released Monday after the government declined to challenge a judge's third order for her release on $100,000 bond.

top.title: Vance publicly defends Trump's Iran war, dismisses rift reports
top.description: The vice president called Trump a "smart president" and said he trusts him to avoid past mistakes, despite his own history of opposing foreign wars.

top.title: EU sanctions 13 over Bucha massacre and Kremlin disinformation
top.description: The measures target nine Russian military officers linked to the 2022 killings and four individuals accused of spreading pro-Kremlin propaganda.

top.title: Rubio designates Iranian American prisoner as wrongfully detained
top.description: Kamran Hekmati, jailed for visiting Israel for his son's bar mitzvah over a decade ago, is now the second American given the formal designation.

top.title: Trump discloses Rep. Dunn's terminal heart diagnosis at White House event
top.description: The president revealed the Florida Republican would have been "dead by June" without emergency surgery, catching Speaker Johnson off guard.

top.title: Drone strike sparks fire at Abu Dhabi's Shah oil field
top.description: The attack is the latest in a series of Iranian strikes on Gulf energy infrastructure since the U.S.-Israel war with Iran began in early March.

top.title: US and Iran reestablish direct contact amid ongoing conflict
top.description: Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi exchanged text messages, the first known direct communication since the war began Feb. 28.

top.title: DOJ proposes rule to help states fast-track executions
top.description: The proposed change would ease certification requirements under a 1996 law that no state has ever successfully used to expedite federal death penalty reviews.

top.title: All five DNA and RNA building blocks found in asteroid Ryugu samples
top.description: A Nature Astronomy study reports the complete set of nucleobases in Ryugu material, making it the second asteroid confirmed to carry life's genetic ingredients.

top.title: Trump says he'll have the 'honor of taking Cuba'
top.description: The president's most aggressive remarks yet on Cuba came during a White House event, as both governments have confirmed ongoing diplomatic talks.

top.title: Cuba to let expatriates invest in and own businesses on the island
top.description: Deputy Prime Minister Fraga announced the reforms in an NBC News interview, as the island faces its worst energy and economic crisis in decades.

top.title: Starmer says Hormuz reopening effort won't be NATO-led
top.description: The UK is building a broad coalition with Gulf, European, and U.S. partners to restore shipping through the strait, which Iran has effectively blockaded.

top.title: Susie Wiles diagnosed with early-stage breast cancer
top.description: Trump announced the diagnosis on Truth Social, saying his chief of staff has an "excellent" prognosis and will keep working during treatment.

top.title: Greece joins Germany in refusing military role at Strait of Hormuz
top.description: Athens said it will only participate in the EU's Red Sea mission as EU foreign ministers meet Monday to discuss expanding the Aspides operation.

top.title: Oxford study identifies new class of molten exoplanet
top.description: L 98-59 d, just 35 light-years away, harbors a permanent magma ocean that stores sulfur and defies existing planetary categories, researchers say.

top.title: Fifth Iranian player abandons asylum, leaving two in Australia
top.description: Only two of seven Iranian women's soccer players who accepted humanitarian visas remain after a fifth member departed overnight Sunday.

top.title: Rare Level 4 severe weather risk targets East Coast as storm outbreak continues Monday
top.description: Tornadoes, damaging winds and blizzards have already destroyed homes and grounded hundreds of flights, with the worst expected Monday in the mid-Atlantic.

top.title: Iran war hits $12B as Tehran rejects ceasefire talks
top.description: White House adviser Kevin Hassett disclosed the cost on Sunday as Iran's foreign minister denied requesting a ceasefire, contradicting Trump's claims.

top.title: 'One Battle After Another' wins best picture at 98th Academy Awards
top.description: Paul Thomas Anderson's political thriller took home six Oscars, including best director and adapted screenplay, in his first Academy Award wins.

top.title: Iran's new supreme leader reportedly flown to Moscow for surgery
top.description: Al-Jarida reports Mojtaba Khamenei was evacuated to Russia on a military aircraft after being wounded in the Feb. 28 strikes that killed his father.

top.title: Trump accuses Iran of using AI to fake war victories, threatens media with treason
top.description: The president claimed Tehran is flooding social media with fabricated images and videos to mask battlefield losses, and said outlets amplifying the content should face charges.

top.title: IDF says Michigan synagogue attacker's brother was Hezbollah commander
top.description: Israel's military disclosed that Ibrahim Ghazali led weapons operations for Hezbollah's Badr Unit before being killed in the same airstrike that preceded the attack.

top.title: House Republicans vow to block Senate bills until SAVE Act advances
top.description: About 40 to 50 House GOP members threaten to halt all Senate legislation unless Thune brings the voter ID bill to a vote.

top.title: China unveils robotic workforce for lunar base construction by 2035
top.description: Researchers proposed a wheeled semi-humanoid robot and a six-legged cargo platform to support the planned International Lunar Research Station.

top.title: Missile hits U.S. Embassy compound in Baghdad as Americans told to leave Iraq
top.description: The State Department urged all U.S. citizens to depart immediately, warning that Iran-aligned militias pose an ongoing threat across Iraq.

top.title: GOP Sen. Johnson breaks with Trump over FCC license threats tied to Iran war coverage
top.description: FCC Chair Brendan Carr's warning that broadcasters could lose licenses over Iran war reporting drew rare bipartisan rebuke, including from a Republican senator.

top.title: North Korea fires 12 nuclear-capable rockets as US-South Korea drills continue
top.description: Kim Jong Un and his daughter oversaw the live-fire drill Saturday, which Pyongyang called 100% accurate amid rising tensions over joint military exercises.

top.title: Families identify airmen killed in KC-135 crash in Iraq
top.description: Maj. Alex Klinner, 33, and Tech. Sgt. Tyler Simmons, 28, are among six crew members killed in the March 12 midair collision during Operation Epic Fury.

top.title: White House tightens control over RFK Jr.'s HHS amid midterm fears
top.description: Aides moved to curb Kennedy's anti-vaccine focus after internal polling found it deeply unpopular, even among MAHA supporters.

top.title: Moscow businesses reel as mobile internet blackout enters second week
top.description: Russia's capital has lost widespread mobile connectivity since March 5, with analysts calling it the largest test yet of a government "whitelist" censorship system.

top.title: EU extends Russia sanctions until September despite Hungary, Slovakia resistance
top.description: Around 2,600 individuals and entities, including Putin, remain subject to travel bans and asset freezes after a last-minute deal.

top.title: MAGA Inc. sits on $300M war chest as GOP awaits Trump's go-ahead
top.description: Republicans worry Trump may not deploy his super PAC's record funds to protect vulnerable House members ahead of November's midterms.

top.title: IDF says it killed two Iranian intelligence chiefs in Tehran airstrike
top.description: Both officers had been appointed after their predecessor was eliminated in earlier strikes during Operation Roaring Lion.

top.title: UAE consulate in Erbil hit by drone for second time in a week
top.description: Two security personnel were wounded in Saturday's strike, which the UAE called an "unprovoked terrorist drone attack" and a violation of international law.

top.title: Lobbyist who helped client get Trump pardon charged with extortion
top.description: Joshua Nass allegedly hired an "enforcer" to threaten the client's son into paying $500,000 he claimed was owed for lobbying services.

top.title: Iranians' regime change hopes fade as bombing enters third week
top.description: Tehran residents who cheered U.S.-Israeli strikes now face a battered but defiant security state and a new supreme leader consolidating power.

top.title: Israeli strikes kill six in Gaza as ceasefire nears fifth month
top.description: Gaza's civil defense agency says a pre-dawn strike in Khan Younis and a separate attack in Gaza City killed six people in the past 24 hours.

top.title: Explosion hits Amsterdam Jewish school in 'deliberate attack'
top.description: Mayor Femke Halsema condemned the overnight blast as a "cowardly act of aggression" amid a wave of attacks on European Jewish sites.

top.title: Over 52,000 flights canceled as Middle East crisis enters third week
top.description: An estimated six million passengers have been affected since the conflict began Feb. 28, with cancellation rates only now starting to ease.

top.title: Hamas urges Iran to stop striking Gulf neighbors
top.description: The Iran-backed group publicly distanced itself from Tehran's regional attacks for the first time since the war began, amid threats of expulsion from Qatar.

top.title: DC-area airports reopen after chemical odor halts flights for hours
top.description: An overheated circuit board at a Virginia air traffic control facility forced ground stops at four airports, causing delays stretching past midnight.

top.title: Greek tanker hit near Russia's Black Sea port
top.description: Greece's shipping minister called the strike on the Maran Homer "unacceptable," vowing protests at the European Council level.

top.title: Revolution Wind delivers first power to New England grid after surviving two Trump shutdowns
top.description: The $6.2 billion offshore wind farm off Rhode Island will supply 350,000 homes at roughly a third of current regional electricity prices.

top.title: Trump blames 'genetics' of attackers after wave of violence
top.description: The president linked recent attacks in New York, Virginia, and Michigan to assailants' genetics, drawing criticism from experts who call the language eugenics-adjacent.

top.title: Iran's UN envoy warns European bases could be targets
top.description: Ambassador Ali Bahreini told Euronews that any facility used to attack Iran, including US bases in Europe, is a "legitimate target."

top.title: Qatar says food reserves can last 18 months amid Iran war
top.description: The interior minister said water supplies cover four months and medicine stocks nine months as Iranian strikes continue across the Gulf.

top.title: USDA says it's close to announcing fertilizer relief for farmers
top.description: Agriculture Secretary Brook Rollins said Friday her department is exploring "every potential avenue" to curb costs as the Iran war leaves a 25% supply gap.

top.title: Cuba says FBI team to help probe deadly speedboat incursion
top.description: Cuban President Díaz-Canel confirmed the FBI will send investigators to Havana, marking rare cooperation amid strained U.S.-Cuba relations.

top.title: UN says nearly all Gaza aid movements denied in past two days
top.description: Only one humanitarian mission was allowed through to the Kerem Shalom crossing on Thursday and Friday, enabling fuel collection "but nothing else."

top.title: Qatar orders evacuations as Gulf conflict enters third week
top.description: Debris from an intercepted drone struck Dubai's financial district Friday as Qatar began evacuating residents from specified areas overnight.

top.title: Colombia sends ministers to Caracas after presidential summit abruptly canceled
top.description: A planned border meeting between Presidents Petro and Rodriguez was called off for undisclosed reasons, shifting talks to the ministerial level.

top.title: Iran-backed Iraqi militia offers bounty on US officials
top.description: The Islamic Resistance in Iraq announced a roughly $114,000 reward for intelligence leading to the capture or killing of senior American personnel.

top.title: Kansas residents split over Deep Fission's underground nuclear reactor project
top.description: Dozens gathered at a March 12 public meeting in Parsons, Kansas, days after the startup began drilling for its first-of-a-kind underground reactor.

top.title: UAE charges 21 people for filming Iranian missile strikes
top.description: Detainees include a British tourist and an Indian student, as the Gulf state cracks down on sharing footage of drone and missile attacks.

top.title: Iranian strike damages 5 US refueling planes at Saudi base
top.description: The planes at Prince Sultan Air Base are being repaired with no casualties reported, according to The Wall Street Journal.

top.title: Schumer demands Bessent testify over Russia sanctions relief
top.description: Senate Democrats condemned the Treasury Department's decision to temporarily lift sanctions on Russian oil stranded at sea amid the Iran conflict.

top.title: US seeks to sell seized tanker after $47M in costs
top.description: Federal prosecutors asked a judge Thursday for permission to sell a confiscated Venezuelan oil tanker and its cargo after maintenance costs far exceeded the ship's value.

top.title: Zelensky calls Ukraine-Russia peace talks a 'soap opera' as venue dispute stalls process
top.description: The U.S., Ukraine and Russia cannot agree on where or when to meet, with the Iran war keeping American officials from traveling abroad.

top.title: South Korea's PM meets Trump after $350B investment bill clears parliament
top.description: The unannounced White House meeting followed the National Assembly's passage of legislation enabling Seoul to fulfill its U.S. investment pledge.

top.title: Gulf states lose $15B in energy revenue as Hormuz blockade nears third week
top.description: The Financial Times reports Saudi Arabia alone has lost $4.5 billion, with $10.7 billion in oil and LNG cargo stranded on ships unable to depart.

top.title: ROTC students killed gunman who attacked Old Dominion University classroom, FBI says
top.description: The FBI is investigating the shooting as terrorism after a convicted ISIS supporter killed an ROTC instructor and wounded two others Thursday.

top.title: Judge blocks Trump from forcing colleges to submit race data
top.description: A federal judge in Boston extended the compliance deadline to March 25 after 17 Democratic state attorneys general sued to halt the requirement.

top.title: Finland won't host nuclear arms in peacetime, Stubb says
top.description: President Stubb sought to ease tensions after Finland's government last week moved to repeal a decades-old ban on nuclear weapons within its borders.

top.title: Over 50,000 Americans return from Middle East since Iran war began
top.description: The State Department said Friday that demand for charter flights is declining as the evacuation effort enters its third week.

top.title: Judge orders indefinite funding for CFPB, rebuking Trump administration
top.description: A California federal judge found officials used flawed legal reasoning in a "transparent attempt" to shut down the consumer watchdog agency.

top.title: Sens. Warren and Van Hollen demand Hegseth's firing over Iran school strike
top.description: The Democratic senators say the defense secretary's rollback of civilian harm protections contributed to the deadly Feb. 28 bombing of an elementary school.

top.title: Judge blocks DOJ subpoenas targeting Fed Chair Powell
top.description: The ruling found "zero evidence" of a crime and called the probe a pretext to pressure Powell on interest rates or force his resignation.

top.title: UAE says Iran must halt attacks before any mediation can begin
top.description: Diplomat Lana Nusseibeh said the guns must go silent first but expressed confidence Trump will lead to a negotiated end to the war.

top.title: Accused drug kingpin Marset arrested in Bolivia, flown to US
top.description: Sebastián Marset, known as "the King of the South" and a top DEA fugitive, was captured in a predawn raid in Santa Cruz de la Sierra.

top.title: Pakistan bombs Kandahar fuel depot, Kabul homes, ending week-long lull
top.description: Overnight strikes killed six people, including children, according to the Taliban, shattering a fragile pause achieved through Chinese mediation.

top.title: Federal judge blocks Trump plan to end TPS for Somali immigrants
top.description: The temporary restraining order preserves legal protections for about 1,100 Somali nationals days before a March 17 termination deadline.

top.title: Midwest farmers rush to sell stored grain as Iran war fuels price surge
top.description: Corn and soybean futures have each climbed roughly 6% since the conflict began, with soybeans topping $12 per bushel for the first time since mid-2024.

top.title: Cuba confirms opening talks with U.S. after months of denial
top.description: President Diaz-Canel said he directed negotiations alongside Raul Castro, marking Havana's first official acknowledgment of discussions with Washington.

top.title: Trump rejected Putin's offer to move Iran's uranium to Russia
top.description: The proposal, made during a March 9 phone call, aimed to resolve the dispute over Iran's enriched uranium stockpile as part of a broader war settlement.

top.title: US military's AI-powered system struck 6,000 targets in Iran as war enters third week
top.description: The Maven Smart System, built by Palantir and powered by Anthropic's Claude, compressed weeks of targeting work into hours amid growing civilian harm concerns.

top.title: Sri Lanka repatriates remains of 84 Iranian sailors killed in US submarine attack
top.description: The bodies were flown from Mattala airport nine days after a US torpedo sank the frigate IRIS Dena off Sri Lanka's southern coast.

top.title: Japan set to join Trump's Golden Dome missile defense program
top.description: PM Takaichi plans to announce Japan's participation when she meets Trump in Washington on March 19, according to Japanese government sources.

top.title: Netanyahu says Israeli strikes killed top Iranian nuclear scientist
top.description: The claim came during his first press conference since the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran began Feb. 28, now in its 14th day.

top.title: Scientists decode 300-million-year-old DNA code hidden in plant genomes
top.description: A massive study of 284 plant species identified 2.3 million conserved regulatory sequences, some predating flowering plants entirely.

top.title: Trump signs orders to cut housing regulations, ease mortgage access
top.description: The executive orders aim to speed up construction permitting and bring community banks back into mortgage lending ahead of the midterms.

top.title: Japan's Katayama signals readiness to act on yen as currency nears 160
top.description: Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said authorities will take "all necessary measures" as the yen hit its weakest level since July 2024 amid surging oil prices.

top.title: ByteDance taps Malaysia partner to deploy 36,000 Nvidia Blackwell chips
top.description: The TikTok parent is working with Aolani Cloud to build AI computing capacity outside China, skirting U.S. export controls on direct chip sales.

top.title: Democrats warn new Trump tariffs will cost households $2,512 this year
top.description: A Joint Economic Committee study released Friday says the post-Supreme Court tariff regime represents a 44% increase over 2025 household costs.

top.title: Florida passes bill requiring proof of citizenship to vote
top.description: The legislation, which won't take effect until 2027, also eliminates student and retirement community IDs as acceptable forms of voter identification.

top.title: Democrats unveil bills to cut taxes for workers, raise them on millionaires
top.description: Federal legislation introduced by Sen. Van Hollen would eliminate income taxes for those earning under $46,000, funded by surtaxes on millionaires.

top.title: GOP senators tell Americans to accept higher gas prices as cost of Iran war
top.description: Gas has surged roughly 20% since the conflict began, with the national average hitting $3.58 a gallon as Strait of Hormuz disruptions roil oil markets.

top.title: NASA clears Artemis II for April 1 moon launch
top.description: Four astronauts will orbit the moon for the first time since Apollo 17, following a flight readiness review that resolved months of technical delays.

top.title: Iran warns regional blackout would follow any US grid strike
top.description: Tehran's top security official said darkness would let Iran "hunt down US servicemen," as threats expanded to oil and energy targets.

top.title: Drones hit Dubai again as Iran's Gulf attacks enter 13th day
top.description: Multiple drone incidents struck Dubai overnight and Thursday morning, including near the financial district, as corporate evacuations spread across the region.

top.title: Aramco denies reports of talks to buy Ukrainian drones
top.description: The Saudi oil giant called a Wall Street Journal report about negotiations with Ukrainian interceptor drone makers "inaccurate," saying no such procurement talks are underway.

top.title: Polish president vetoes €44B EU defense loan bill
top.description: President Nawrocki rejected legislation unlocking EU funds under the SAFE program, calling it a threat to sovereignty and sparking a clash with PM Tusk.

top.title: Iran's UN envoy says strait won't close, contradicting new supreme leader
top.description: Mojtaba Khamenei's first statement vowed to keep the Strait of Hormuz shut, but Iran's UN ambassador said hours later Tehran would not block the waterway.

top.title: Israel using tips from Iranian civilians to guide Tehran drone strikes
top.description: A senior Israeli official says ordinary Iranians are submitting target locations via social media, helping guide strikes on Basij checkpoints in Tehran.

top.title: Greene says GOP 'hijacked' by hawks as MAGA splits over Iran war
top.description: The former congresswoman accused party leaders of enabling a neoconservative takeover, deepening fractures within Trump's coalition over the two-week-old conflict.

top.title: US envoy rebukes Czechs for falling short of NATO spending target
top.description: Czech lawmakers approved a 2026 budget allocating just 1.7% of GDP to defense, well below NATO's 5% goal agreed at The Hague Summit.

top.title: Israel says it struck nuclear weapons site in Tehran
top.description: The IDF said the Taleghan compound was tied to Iran's covert AMAD nuclear weapons program, as the war enters its third week.

top.title: US reopens DEA office in Dominican Republic after corruption probe
top.description: The Santo Domingo office was shuttered in February after a senior agent was arrested on bribery and visa fraud charges.

top.title: Canada says Iran war will dominate upcoming G7 foreign ministers' meeting
top.description: Foreign Affairs Minister Anita Anand said G7 allies will hold "a full-fledged conversation" on diplomatic efforts when they gather near Paris on March 26-27.

top.title: Israel attacked Iran with no regime change plan, security sources say
top.description: Nearly two weeks into the U.S.-Israeli bombing campaign, senior Israeli officials admit hopes of a popular uprising were based on "wishful thinking."

top.title: Israel drops charges against soldiers in Sde Teiman sexual abuse case
top.description: Netanyahu praised the decision, saying Israel "should pursue its adversaries, not its heroic fighters." Rights groups condemned it as impunity.

top.title: Israeli drone strike on Lebanese University kills two senior academics
top.description: The faculty of sciences director and a professor were killed on the Hadath campus near Beirut's southern suburbs amid intensified Israeli bombardment across Lebanon.
